# Portfolio Development Guidelines

## Performance & Best Practices
- Minimize DOM manipulations - batch updates when possible
- Lazy load images and defer non-critical JS
- Use CSS transforms over position changes for animations
- Avoid inline styles - use classes
- Minify and compress assets for production

## Accessibility
- Use semantic HTML elements
- Include proper alt text for images
- Ensure keyboard navigation works
- Use ARIA labels where appropriate
- Maintain color contrast ratios (WCAG AA minimum)

## Design Philosophy
- Maintain a distinct, personal visual identity — avoid generic portfolio templates
- Create custom UI components rather than copying popular portfolio designs
- Use creative, unexpected interactions that reflect my personality
- Design should feel like *mine*, not like every other developer portfolio

## Style & Tone
- Professional but not corporate
- Clean and modern, but with personality
- Balance between minimalism and visual interest
- Color scheme: Blue (#2E5BFF) and dark navy (#080D1A) as primary
- Typography: Space Grotesk (already in use) — maintain consistency

## Code Approach
- Write original implementations, don't copy-paste common portfolio patterns
- If suggesting a feature, build it from scratch rather than using generic snippets
- Explain *why* a design choice works, not just *how* to implement it
- Prioritize performance and maintainability over flashy effects

## What NOT to Do
- Don't suggest generic "scroll animations" or "particle backgrounds" unless specifically requested
- Don't copy UI patterns from popular portfolios (Brittany Chiang, etc.) — use them as inspiration only
- Don't use overused phrases like "passionate developer" or "problem solver"
- Don't suggest adding features just because they're trendy

## When Making Suggestions
- Always consider: "Does this reflect Siyabonga's actual work and personality?"
- Prioritize substance over style — real projects > flashy animations
- If improving existing code, maintain the current architecture and patterns
- Ask clarifying questions if a request could be interpreted multiple ways
